description: Trusted reference corpus for TruthLens. Each entry is a single factual sentence with a topic tag and a short source label. Used as the evidence pool for TF-IDF retrieval.

sentences.text: The mitochondria is the organelle that produces most of the cell's ATP through aerobic respiration.
sentences.source: Alberts, Molecular Biology of the Cell

sentences.text: Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen using light energy.
DNA is composed of four nucleotide bases: adenine, thymine, guanine, and cytosine.
Human red blood cells lack a nucleus when mature and carry oxygen using hemoglobin.
Ribosomes are the cellular structures responsible for synthesizing proteins from messenger RNA.
sentences.source: Alberts, Molecular Biology of the Cell

sentences.text: The human body has 206 bones in the adult skeleton.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure of one atmosphere at sea level.
Water freezes into ice at 0 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
The chemical formula for water is H2O, made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
Gold is a chemical element with the symbol Au and atomic number 79.
An acid is a substance with a pH below 7, while a base has a pH above 7.
The pH scale ranges from 0 to 14, with 7 being neutral at 25 degrees Celsius.
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second.
Sound travels at roughly 343 meters per second in dry air at 20 degrees Celsius.
Newton's first law states that an object in motion stays in motion unless acted on by an external force.
Gravity on Earth accelerates falling objects at approximately 9.8 meters per second squared.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed, according to the law of conservation of energy.
The Great Wall of China is not visible to the unaided human eye from low Earth orbit, contrary to popular belief.
The Sun is a star located at the center of our Solar System.
Earth completes one orbit around the Sun in approximately 365.25 days.
The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite and orbits the planet about every 27.3 days.
Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun and is often called the Red Planet.
There are eight planets in the Solar System after Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level on Earth, standing about 8,849 meters tall.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceans.
The Nile is widely regarded as the longest river in the world, flowing through northeastern Africa.
The capital of France is Paris.
The Amazon rainforest is the largest tropical rainforest, located mostly in Brazil.
The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world, covering much of North Africa.
Python is a dynamically typed, interpreted programming language created by Guido van Rossum.
HTTP is a stateless application-layer protocol used for transferring web documents.
Binary search runs in logarithmic time on a sorted array.
RAM is volatile memory that loses its contents when power is removed.
A compiler translates source code into machine code before the program runs.
sentences.source: Aho, Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools

sentences.text: World War II ended in 1945 with the surrender of Germany and Japan.
The Declaration of Independence of the United States was adopted in 1776.
The Berlin Wall fell in 1989, marking a key moment in the end of the Cold War.
